page: Microsoft Azure B16als_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Basv2 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The server features 16 virtual CPUs mapped from 8 physical cores with multithreading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. It is configured with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include any local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the B16als_v2 demonstrates strong single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 25% of tested servers via stress-ng. Its multi-core CPU performance, measured by stress-ng and PassMark CPU tests, falls within the average range. Memory performance is notable, with PassMark memory scores ranking in the top 25%, while raw memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks remain in the average tier. Additionally, the server achieves strong results in text generation for small language models, though prompt processing and larger model inference perform at average speeds.

The qualitative cost profile of the B16als_v2 is defined by its burstable CPU allocation and low memory-to-vCPU ratio. This resource density makes it a cost-efficient option for workloads that experience variable CPU utilization rather than sustained, continuous high loads. However, the lack of local storage and the burstable nature of the CPU represent clear tradeoffs for applications requiring guaranteed, non-throttled compute power or high-speed local disk access. This server is suited for workloads such as static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and light text processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure B16als_v2 is an x86_64 burstable virtual machine from the Basv2 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. It features 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyperthreading and 32.0 GB of RAM, representing a low-memory configuration of 2.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show strong single-core CPU and PassMark memory performance, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth results. The server also demonstrates strong text generation speeds for small language models, though prompt processing and larger model inference remain average. Due to its burstable CPU allocation and low memory density, it offers a cost-efficient profile for variable workloads. It is suited for static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and light text processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B16als_v2 is an x86_64 burstable VM featuring 16 vCPUs, 32.0 GB of RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It offers strong single-core performance for variable workloads without local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B16als_v2 is a burstable x86_64 VM with 16 vCPUs, 32.0 GB RAM, and an AMD EPYC CPU, optimized for variable workloads.
tagline: A burstable, low-memory virtual machine featuring strong single-core performance and AMD EPYC processors for variable, cost-conscious workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor at 3.525 GHz
16 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM with a 2.0 GB per core ratio
Burstable CPU allocation designed for variable workloads
Strong single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
No local storage with Premium Storage capability
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and database operations
